ask_next.question: Can the person make any sound, cough, or speak at all?
ask_next.purpose: Distinguish complete from partial airway obstruction
ask_next.why: Complete obstruction is immediately life-threatening; partial can often be self-cleared

response_guides.note: Complete airway obstruction — begin back blows and abdominal thrusts immediately
Partial obstruction — encourage coughing, standby to assist if worsens to silent

ask_next.question: Is the person still conscious and standing, or have they gone limp or unconscious?
ask_next.purpose: Determine if CPR transition is needed
ask_next.why: Unconscious choking requires different protocol — lower to ground and start CPR

response_guides.note: Lower carefully to ground. Start CPR. Check mouth for visible object before each breath attempt.

ask_next.question: What did they choke on, and how long ago did it start?
ask_next.why: Duration and object type help EMS prepare and set priority

response_guides.note: Prolonged obstruction — hypoxia risk increasing, escalate immediately
